title: Decoder GPT-2 bf16/PWL tie-rank hardware frontier
hypothesis: The GPT-2 96-prompt stress run showed bf16/PWL misses are recoverable with logit tie-rank. Before starting QAT, consume the merged score-tie-rank datapath PPA and check whether that recovery path is hardware-plausible under the current Nangate45 row-8 component model.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Is the GPT-2 bf16/PWL logit tie-rank recovery path backed by measured hardware component PPA rather than only software post-processing?
include: merged GPT-2 prompt-stress bf16/PWL recovery summary
quality gate that requires recovered next-token and top-k exactness with zero regressions

expected_benefit: separates hardware-plausible recovery from software-only tie-break recovery
decides whether QAT is the immediate next step or can wait for larger-scale stress
keeps the next job small by reusing merged evidence
risks: the additive component model is conservative and not a full integrated macro
the measured datapaths are row-8 Nangate45 blocks, not production-scale array evidence
the GPT-2 prompt-stress set is still a next-token screen rather than full application evaluation

required_evaluations.objective: Check whether GPT-2 bf16/PWL logit tie-rank recovery is hardware-plausible using merged reciprocal-normalization and score-tie-rank PPA before starting QAT.
